MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: FURTHER HEARING TO SELECT AN ARBITRATOR AND ALTERNATIVE ARBITRATORS. (SEE CCP 1281.6.).

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court appoints Hon. Glenda Sanders (Ret.) to serve as the parties' arbitrator. In the event of unavailability or disqualification pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 1281.91, Hon. Fred K. Morrison (Ret.) shall be the first alternate arbitrator and Hon. Vedica Puri (Ret.) shall be the second alternate arbitrator.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Jul-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
**Jeffrey Kiburtz, Esq. Cousnel for Petitioner appeared on ZOOM. Counsel states that the tentative ruling was not posted online. Clerk checked the court's website and the tentative ruling for this motion was posted on 7/22/25. The motion was not properly contested by the 4 p.m. cut-off time to contest. The matter is not called. *

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

Petitioner San Francisco Unified School District petitions to compel arbitration of its dispute with the Northern California Regional Liability Excess Fund (NCR) pursuant to CCP 1281.2. The court grants the petition on terms discussed below.

SFUSD has met its burden of presenting "prima facie evidence of a written agreement to arbitrate the controversy." (Ramirez v. Golden Queen Mining Company, LLC (2024) 102 Cal.App.5th 821, 830; see also Iyere v. Wise Auto Group (2023) 87 Cal.App.5th 747, 755; Ex. 1 to Kiburtz Dec.) NCR presents no evidence to create a factual dispute about the existence of the arbitration agreement and raises no argument that it does not cover this dispute. Rather, NCR's briefing is devoted to the merits of its underlying dispute with SFUSD, which is not at issue on this motion, and to the qualifications of the arbitrator it prefers.

Because SFUSD has met its burden, the court grants the petition and compels arbitration. The arbitration agreement does not provide a method of appointing an arbitrator and the parties have not agreed to a single neutral arbitrator. (Kiburtz Dec., Ex. 1, p. 23.) The court orders the parties to exchange at least five names each by July 30, 2025 and to agree on an arbitrator if possible by August 6, 2025. If the parties are unable to agree, each is ordered to submit three names to the court in simultaneous supplemental filings by August 8, 2025. The court sets the matter for further hearing on August 13, 2025 and will select an arbitrator and alternative arbitrators at that hearing. (See CCP 1281.6.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in cop,laince with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Jun-23-2025 Continued To Jul-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
